published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML: DML product-rate ATE inference; LRO/ADML papers supply analogous orthogonal-EIF pieces individually

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest analogues are eid_bjs_efficient_event_study: quotient IF frontier for staggered DiD, eid_lp_svar_nonequiv: non-equivalence margin warning, and pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec: joint nuisance geometry; none is an ExactID ATE DML/LRO/ADML spectral frontier

published_prior_art.cite: none; checked DML, LRO, ADML, RR, Newey-Robins cross-fitting, Avagyan-Vansteelandt, Dukes-Vansteelandt-Whitney, Bruns-Smith et al., and Biometrika DML theorem

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv and eid_bjs_efficient_event_study motivate primitive witnesses but do not give this finite-support ATE estimator-separation witness

published_prior_art.cite: BrunsSmithDukesFellerOgburn2024ABW: algebraic equivalences for augmented balancing weights/AutoDML, but not a finite-support DML-LRO-ADML variance-separation witness

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The observable kernel K_n is not dimensionally pinned: U_n U_n^T and alpha_n alpha_n^T are added without specifying a common coordinate/projection, and alpha_n is called a function of X while containing D.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The below-frontier variance inequalities omit the rate/amplitude factor linking r_g+r_p to lambda_min^+(K_n); as written the lower bound scales only with lambda_min and does not encode the proposed rho_star threshold.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define K_n as a covariance operator on an explicit finite gap-coordinate basis, with projection maps for DML, LRO, and ADML and a precise plug-in observability claim.

Replace the variance-gap display by a rate-scaled lower bound, e.g. involving n^{1-2(r_g+r_p)} lambda_min^+(K_n) or the corresponding normalized local-amplitude parameter, so the iff frontier and separation claim match.

Add a compact comparison table against Avagyan-Vansteelandt, Dukes-Vansteelandt-Whitney, and Bruns-Smith et al. separating one-sided robustness, nonregularity, algebraic equivalence, and the proposed triple-estimator spectral boundary.
Pin the LRO ATE specialization by an explicit Jacobian/projection matrix and state the exact condition under which its span equals the ADML minimal Riesz span.

recommended_next_step: Patch the kernel definition and the rate-scaled variance-gap statement, then rerun novelty review; the novelty target remains plausibly flagship after those fixes.
